page: OVHcloud b3-64 is a General Purpose server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, and the system is configured with one thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance has a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps along with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, placing the instance in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for large blocks, both read-only and write, rank in the top 25%, indicating strong throughput for larger data sets, though small-block cached reads remain average. Single-threaded and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside PassMark physics and memory tests, also register in the top 25%. For language model workloads, prompt processing and text generation using a small 135M model achieve strong performance, while a medium 7B model yields average speeds.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and balanced RAM-to-vCPU ratio offer consistent resource density without hyperthreading overhead. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and a public IPv4 address reduces the need for separate storage or networking add-ons. Appropriate workloads for this configuration include general-purpose applications, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations.
description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a General Purpose server instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Milan x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Hyperthreading is disabled, providing a direct one-to-one mapping of vCPUs to physical cores. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10%, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth and bzip3 decompression speeds. Multi-core CPU metrics and database operations fall within the average range. This hardware profile is suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a General Purpose server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a General Purpose server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC Milan instance featuring balanced compute and memory resources with local NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with hyperthreading disabled
64.0 GB of RAM and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address included
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations
